keyIdeas: The core mole equation is n = m / M, where n is amount of substance (mol), m is mass (g), and M is molar mass (g molвҒ»В№).
For gases at RTP (25 В°C, 100 kPa): n = V(cmВі) / 24,000 or equivalently n = V(dmВі) / 24.
The ideal gas equation pV = nRT links pressure (Pa), volume (mВі), amount (mol), and temperature (K); R = 8.314 J molвҒ»В№ KвҒ»В№.
Unit conversions required for pV = nRT: pressure must be in Pa (1 kPa = 1000 Pa; 1 atm = 101,325 Pa), volume in mВі (1 dmВі = 10вҒ»Ві mВі; 1 cmВі = 10вҒ»вҒ¶ mВі), temperature in K (K = В°C + 273).
All three equations are rearrangeable: m = nM; V = n Г— 24,000; n = pV / RT.
Multi-step calculations chain these equations вҖ” identify which equation applies at each step and carry through unrounded values.

mustKnow.definition: Amount of substance (mol) equals mass (g) divided by molar mass (g molвҒ»В№).
24,000 cmВі molвҒ»В№ (24 dmВі molвҒ»В№) at 25 В°C and 100 kPa.
pV = nRT where p is pressure in Pa, V is volume in mВі, n is amount in mol, R = 8.314 J molвҒ»В№ KвҒ»В№, T is temperature in K.
8.314 J molвҒ»В№ KвҒ»В№ (provided in OCR data booklet).

markSchemePhrases: n = V / 24,000 (V in cmВі) OR n = V / 24 (V in dmВі)
pressure in Pa, volume in mВі
commandWords: 'Calculate' requires a numerical answer with correct units and full working вҖ” every substitution must be shown for method marks. 'Show that' means arrive at the given answer with clear working; do not just state the answer. 'Deduce' asks you to derive a value or conclusion from given data.
examinerTips: Always convert temperature to K and pressure to Pa before substituting into pV = nRT вҖ” forgetting this costs all marks on a multi-mark calculation.
Show every substitution explicitly: write the equation, substitute values with units, then calculate вҖ” method marks are awarded even if the final answer is wrong.
If a gas volume is given in dmВі and you want to use the molar gas volume, divide by 24 (dmВі); if in cmВі, divide by 24,000.
Carry extra significant figures through intermediate steps and round only the final answer to match the data (usually 3 s.f.).
For ideal gas calculations, R = 8.314 J molвҒ»В№ KвҒ»В№ is in the data booklet вҖ” never memorise a wrong value.
workedExample:
A sample of sulfur dioxide (SOвӮӮ, M = 64.1 g molвҒ»В№) has a volume of 560 cmВі at a temperature of 127 В°C and a pressure of 200 kPa. Calculate the mass of SOвӮӮ.

Step 1: Convert units.
T = 127 + 273 = 400 K
p = 200 Г— 10Ві = 2.00 Г— 10вҒө Pa
V = 560 Г— 10вҒ»вҒ¶ = 5.60 Г— 10вҒ»вҒҙ mВі

Step 2: Calculate n using pV = nRT.
n = pV / RT = (2.00 Г— 10вҒө Г— 5.60 Г— 10вҒ»вҒҙ) / (8.314 Г— 400)
n = 112 / 3325.6 = 3.367 Г— 10вҒ»ВІ mol

Step 3: Calculate mass.
m = nM = 3.367 Г— 10вҒ»ВІ Г— 64.1 = 2.16 g (3 s.f.)
commonTraps: Forgetting to convert В°C to K вҖ” using T = 127 instead of 400 K gives an answer 2.3Г— too small.
Keeping volume in cmВі or dmВі when using pV = nRT вҖ” must convert to mВі.
Keeping pressure in kPa when using pV = nRT вҖ” must convert to Pa.
Rounding intermediate values too early, causing rounding error in the final answer.
Using 22,400 cmВі molвҒ»В№ (STP) instead of 24,000 cmВі molвҒ»В№ (RTP) for gas volumes.
